probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 14%: compound stress now at ELEVATED with Brent near CRITICAL, risk-off dominating, Fear & Greed at 18. XRP at $1.36 is already approaching the <$1 bear threshold. Base case reduced from 40% to 43% (widened slightly as the most likely moderate-outcome range). Mid case increased from 38% to 32% — macro headwinds make the $8-20 range harder to reach in 2026. Bull case trimmed from 12% to 11%. Net shift: increased probability weight on downside scenarios reflecting the materially deteriorated macro environment. The compound stress matrix moving to ELEVATED is a structural change, not noise.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Elevated from prior assessment. Macro headwinds are now directly threatening the position with XRP at $1.36 (36% above bear kill threshold of <$1). Compound stress at ELEVATED for first time. BTC losing $110B despite institutional adoption news proves macro is dominating fundamentals — the same dynamic applies to XRP. On-chain activity gap (RLUSD: $355M on XRPL vs $1.589B total) raises real questions about XRPL-specific adoption.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT's incremental upgrades continue serving 11,500+ banks. No evidence of institutional migration away from SWIFT to XRPL for core settlement — SWIFT remains default.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new competitive signals. Visa B2B Connect serves a different segment than institutional settlement but remains a viable alternative for corporate cross-border payments.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ripple positioning as digital prime broker alongside JPMorgan (per Ripple Prime white paper) suggests parallel rather than competitive positioning, but JPMorgan's captive network advantage with existing banking clients is significant.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS Nexus remains the most credible threat — sovereign-backed, multi-central-bank coordination, explicitly designed to enable cross-border settlement without private bridge currencies. No evidence Nexus requires or will integrate XRPL.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: The RLUSD supply divergence ($1.234B on Ethereum vs $355M on XRPL) is itself evidence that Ethereum is capturing more institutional stablecoin activity than XRPL. Ondo Finance pushing tokenized stocks on Ethereum adds to institutional Ethereum gravity.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for an extended period with no timeline for restoration — the lack of transparency itself is a bearish signal, as growing volume would presumably be publicized.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity data available. XRP 24h volume at $2.49B against $83.4B market cap gives a crude velocity ratio of ~3% daily turnover — but without hold-time data, utility vs speculation split remains unknown.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: $805B equity wipeout in a single session, Fear & Greed at 18, compound stress at ELEVATED — recession signals are accumulating and would severely suppress institutional risk appetite for new infrastructure adoption.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: BTC losing $110B despite institutional adoption news is a warning sign. XRP at $1.36 is 80%+ below prior cycle highs. Extreme Fear at 18 is approaching crypto winter territory but ETF infrastructure provides structural floor that didn't exist in prior winters.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: House-Senate CBDC ban friction shows legislative process is contentious but crypto-favorable administration remains in place. SEC/CFTC co-location signals alignment. Risk is low but Clarity Act advancement remains stalled at PENDING.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 4.13% with DXY at 98.96 (weak dollar + high rates is an unusual combination suggesting capital outflow dynamics). Fed path unclear but BOJ trapped position creates asymmetric rate differential pressure on carry trades.

bear_case.bear_narrative: INCREASE MONITORING. Compound stress escalated from MONITORING to ELEVATED in three days — Brent surged 17.7% to $93.02, now $2 from CRITICAL threshold, while USD/JPY holds at 157.92. Three kill switches escalated to WARNING: ODL volume remains a SUSPICIOUS ABSENCE (the thesis's primary metric is unverifiable and Ripple's cessation of publication is the sharpest bear signal in the analysis), RLUSD circulation pace is insufficient with only 22% on XRPL, and BIS sovereign interop momentum is building. The central paradox is unresolved and must be stated honestly: Ripple's behavior says the numbers disappoint while institutional fiduciary behavior says they're adequate — I don't know which is right, and forcing a resolution without tie-breaking evidence would be a structural error per [LZ-EH-005]. The operator's action window is measured in days, not weeks, driven by Brent's proximity to the CRITICAL threshold.
